figures.caption: Fig. 1. Comparison between the SoundSpaces baseline and our method.

Fig. 2. SACF for audio-visual navigation receives image and audio observations ot = {It, Dt, At} at each time step. Features are extracted via visual/audio encoders, and spatial descriptors gt are generated by SDLD. Subsequently, ACVF conditions on [Fa t ; gt] to generate FiLM-style channel modulation parameters that enhance the directionality of visual representations. These fused features are input into a GRU, which outputs action at and value estimates via Actor-Critic for closed-loop decision-making.

Fig. 3. SDLD fuses F v t and Fa t into Fav to predict distance/angle distributions, then takes the expectation to obtain d̂, θ̂. These are encoded as compact spatial descriptors using (cos θ̂, sin θ̂).
